{{Quote|Way I heard it, nine guys broke out, one of them a Jedi.|Anonymous Imperial CPO to [[Tenn Graneet]]|<ref name="Death Star (novel)">[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']]</ref>}}
The '''Death Star prisoner''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Surviving the end of the war, the disbanding of the Order, and the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Jedi was imprisoned aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] as of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], he was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Surviving the violence of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and the disbanding of the Jedi Order by the traitorous [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the Jedi went to ground as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] initiated the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. Remaining hidden for nearly two decades, the Jedi was imprisoned by the Empire on the secret [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]].<ref name="BFII" />

Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] the Jedi rallied various prisoners of [[Detention Block 2180]] including those members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] who had been captured during an [[Battle of Naboo (Imperial Period)|operation]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The [[Death Star Uprising|uprising]] saw prisoners escape from their cells and raid one of the station's armories. As various prisoners scattered throughout the station, the Jedi led a large group to [[Hangar 85G]] where they attempted to board a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]] and almost succeeded with less than 2 minutes before take-off. Their Jedi leader at last exposed himself in a desperate sprint toward the shuttle, but a moment later [[Imperial shock trooper/Legends|Imperial shock troopers]] of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] were able to destroy the craft.<ref name="BFII" />

The frenzied Jedi, who had managed to obtain two [[Color/Legends|green]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], cut through all of the troopers that stepped into his path. After a horrific firefight, he was gunned down. As a result of the break, the troopers of the 501st were re-assigned in the search for the stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]].<ref name="BFII" />

===Legacy===
While unsuccessful, the uprising was actually a "blessing in disguise." Despite the Jedi's death, several rebels escaped the prison and were able to smuggle the plans off the station and deliver them into the hands of the Rebel Alliance leadership. Not long after, the space station was [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|blown up]] by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="BFII" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character is credited as Rebel Leader in the game.
